subject: Geography
title: What Is a Border?
description: Learn What Is a Border? in one minute.

hook.text: There are over 300,000 km of international borders on Earth, yet none exist in nature.

buildup.text: Borders are invisible agreements—lines where laws, languages, and currencies change.

discovery.text: Some follow rivers or mountains. Others cut straight through deserts with a ruler.

twist.text: Step across a line and your rights, taxes, and even the time on your watch may change.

climax.text: Borders are humanity's most powerful imaginary lines—invisible, yet worth dying for.

punchline.text: The most powerful lines on Earth don't actually exist.

quiz.question: What is true about international borders?
options: They are human-made agreements, not natural features
They are all based on rivers
They have existed since the dawn of life
